title: Configure a DestinationRule circuit breaker
description: Set connectionPool limits and outlierDetection on a DestinationRule so unhealthy upstream hosts eject and concurrency is capped.
body: Use when an upstream needs protection from overload or flaky instances. Do not leave the pool unbounded or eject the entire fleet. Actions: on the DestinationRule set connectionPool (max connections, http1MaxPendingRequests, maxRequestsPerConnection) to bound concurrency and outlierDetection (consecutive5xxErrors, interval, baseEjectionTime, maxEjectionPercent) to eject bad hosts while keeping a quorum. Test with an injected error stream. Validation signals include the bad host being ejected and the breaker tripping when the pool saturates. Risk boundary: tightening these on a live service can shed real traffic and is human-gated for production.
